message:
So last week we had that unexpected water main break three blocks over, and suddenly half the neighborhood lost pressure. Nothing catastrophic, but enough to make you think.

I watched our family group chat blow up in real time. My sister asking if she should boil water. My mom remembering we had some cases in the garage (??). My brother just sending a gif of someone panicking.

Turns out we *did* have supplies—scattered across three different closets, some of it expired, zero documentation of what or where. Not exactly the vibe.

It hit me that preparedness isn't about being that person with the apocalypse bunker. It's just... knowing your own stuff. Where the water is. What's actually good. Whether everyone in the house knows the plan. The boring stuff.

The group chat calmed down after like two hours when everything came back online. But I spent the next evening just doing a little quiet audit with my family. Took maybe an hour total. Now when chaos pops up, we can actually *act* instead of spiraling.

Does your family have a system for this kind of thing, or is it more of a "we'll figure it out if we have to" setup?
